title: Shared presentational components — badge, icon, states, indicator
purpose: Provide the small, reused presentational vocabulary the views depend on — status badge, event icon, the three async-state panels, and the connection indicator — so every view renders consistent loading, empty, error, and connection feedback.
task: Build the shared components: StatusBadge (WorkflowStatus → badge), EventIcon (event kind → icon + colour), EmptyState, ErrorState (with retry), LoadingSkeleton, and ConnectionIndicator (driven by useConnectionStatus). These are the reusable building blocks AU-004, AU-005, and AU-007 consume.

requirements.title: Status badge and event icon
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a StatusBadge mapping each WorkflowStatus to a labelled, colour-coded badge and an EventIcon mapping each event kind to an icon and colour.
acceptance: StatusBadge renders a distinct colour/label for Running, Completed, Failed, Cancelled, and TimedOut.
EventIcon renders a distinct icon and colour for lifecycle, activity, timer, signal, and child-workflow kinds (lucide-react icons, matching apps/web).
A test asserts each WorkflowStatus and each event kind maps to a distinct rendering.

requirements.title: Empty, error, and loading state panels
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide reusable EmptyState (message + optional icon), ErrorState (cause message + retry affordance), and LoadingSkeleton (layout-reserving skeleton) components.
acceptance: EmptyState renders a configurable message and optional icon.
ErrorState renders a cause message and a retry button that invokes a supplied callback.
LoadingSkeleton renders layout-reserving skeletons (configurable for list rows and timeline entries), not a bare spinner.
A test asserts ErrorState's retry invokes the callback and the three panels render distinctly.

requirements.title: Connection indicator
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a ConnectionIndicator that renders the live connection state from useConnectionStatus (connected, reconnecting, disconnected).
acceptance: ConnectionIndicator consumes useConnectionStatus and renders a distinct visual for connected, reconnecting, and disconnected.
It is always present in the layout (it does not disappear when connected; it shows a steady 'connected' affordance).
A test drives the three states and asserts the matching rendering.

boundaries: SHALL NOT define useConnectionStatus — that is AU-003 (this brief consumes it).
SHALL NOT build the list or detail views — AU-004, AU-005 (this brief provides the components they consume).
verification: bun test passes for the component tests; no lint-disable comments added.
